title: Functions, Callbacks, Context, and Memory Interfaces · Object-Lifetime Lens: Out Parameter Transaction
summary: A plugin registry invokes callbacks after their contexts change ownership and one implementation uses an incompatible signature. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem out parameter transaction increment in typed callback and context registry. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Leave out-parameters in documented states on success and every failure, with ownership transfer made explicit.
Inject matched allocate, resize, and release operations with context and failure semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Out Parameter Transaction · Theory Out Parameter Transaction
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 5173. Produce bounded C evidence to bind callback results to explicit context lifetime; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
